key_positions: Commit to peaking carbon emissions before 2030 and achieving carbon neutrality by 2060.
Massively expand renewable energy capacity, particularly solar and wind, as the world leader in green tech manufacturing.
Uphold the principle of 'Common But Differentiated Responsibilities' (CBDR) for developing nations.
Invest in modernizing the industrial sector for higher energy efficiency.
Strengthen South-South cooperation on climate change.
red_lines: Opposes any measures that infringe upon national sovereignty or economic development rights.
Rejects attempts by developed nations to shift the burden of historical emissions onto developing countries.
Demands significant financial and technological support from developed nations as part of international agreements.
